title: Education Policy Political Spending, Lobbying, and Influence

student_loan_industry.overview: The student loan industry spends millions annually lobbying against reform while servicing $1.7T+ in outstanding federal student debt. Major servicers like Navient, MOHELA, and Nelnet maintain robust lobbying operations to influence repayment policies, forgiveness programs, and servicer accountability rules.

total_student_debt.yoy_growth_2025: $54 billion increase; quarterly YoY growth averaged 2.94%
total_student_debt.federal_share: Federal student loan debt makes up more than 90% of all student loan debt
total_student_debt.source: U.S. Department of Education / Education Data Initiative

industry_lobbying_spending.trend: Industry spent over $50 million lobbying Congress since 2008

navient.role: Largest student loan company; top lobbying spender since 2015
navient.note: Navient's 2021 spending was its lowest annual total in 12 years

sallie_mae.note: SLM Corp (Sallie Mae successor) continues active lobbying presence

mohela.federal_payments_since_2011: Over $1.1 billion in DOE payments for servicing
mohela.supreme_court_role: Central to Biden v. Nebraska (2023) — standing for Missouri's lawsuit was based on MOHELA's potential revenue loss from mass loan cancellation
mohela.accountability_issues: Over 50 members of Congress called on DOE to consider terminating MOHELA contract in 2024; investigated by multiple state attorneys general

nelnet.note: Acquired Great Lakes in 2018; now services federal loans under its own brand

biden_student_loan_forgiveness_battles.initial_plan: Broad forgiveness of up to $20,000 per borrower under HEROES Act

supreme_court_ruling.outcome: 6-3 ruling struck down plan; Court invoked 'major questions doctrine' finding Biden exceeded HEROES Act authority
supreme_court_ruling.standing_basis: Missouri had standing because MOHELA (state-chartered servicer) would lose revenue when forgiven loans left its portfolio

second_attempt_blocked.detail: Seven GOP-led states, led by Missouri, pre-emptively sued to block Biden's new debt relief plan; U.S. District Court for Southern District of Georgia issued temporary restraining order within 2 days

biden_student_loan_forgiveness_battles.total_forgiven_by_biden: Approximately $175 billion for 4.76 million borrowers through targeted programs (PSLF, IDR adjustments, borrower defense)
biden_student_loan_forgiveness_battles.percentage_of_total_debt: ~9.5% of all federal student debt canceled

for_profit_college_industry.overview: The for-profit college industry mounted one of the largest lobbying surges in education history during 2010-2012 when Obama-era gainful employment rules threatened their access to federal student aid. Industry spending declined after major closures (Corinthian 2015, ITT Tech 2016) but has continued through regulatory tug-of-war across three administrations.

lobbying_spending.2010: $7.57 million (nearly 3x increase after gainful employment proposed)
lobbying_spending.2011_peak: $12.5 million (158 lobbyists from 37 firms; 71% had prior government experience; 12 former members of Congress)
lobbying_spending.source: OpenSecrets / Sunlight Foundation / Higher Ed Dive

corinthian_colleges.reason: Denied access to federal funds after investigations into fraudulent practices
corinthian_colleges.loan_discharge: $5.8 billion for 560,000 borrowers (June 2022 — single largest student loan discharge in history)

devry_university.lobbyist: Heather Podesta lobbied for DeVry from 2010-2015
devry_university.enforcement: $37 million in loans canceled for former students

apscu_trade_group.lobbying_by_trent_lott_firm: $1.44 million over 4 years (ending late 2015) via Squire Patton Boggs
apscu_trade_group.lobbyists: Former Senate Majority Leader Trent Lott and former Senator John Breaux

obama_era.first_version: 2010 — set debt-to-income rate minimums for vocational programs
obama_era.industry_response: Massive lobbying surge; industry employed 158 lobbyists in 2014; campaign contributions exceeded $4 million

trump_era.action: Betsy DeVos rescinded gainful employment rule entirely in July 2019
trump_era.impact: For-profit colleges retained billions in federal student aid they would have lost
trump_era.devos_connections: Staffed DOE with former for-profit college executives

biden_era.proposed: May 2023 — new gainful employment regulations released
biden_era.final_rule: October 2023 — restored accountability with debt-to-earnings requirements
biden_era.industry_lobbying: Intense opposition from for-profit college associations
biden_era.trump_2025: Reporting deadlines delayed; rule implementation uncertain under new administration

actions: Staffed Education Department with former for-profit college executives
Rolled back borrower defense to repayment rule
Delayed enforcement of fraud findings against for-profit institutions

charter_school_lobbying.overview: Charter school advocacy is backed by some of America's wealthiest families, with the Walton Family Foundation and DeVos family providing hundreds of millions in education philanthropy and political spending. Pro-charter organizations maintain robust lobbying operations at state and federal levels.

walton_family_foundation.total_education_investment: Over $1 billion to date in education (charter, district, and private schools)
walton_family_foundation.charter_school_funding_since_1996: Over $400 million in charter school grants
walton_family_foundation.schools_funded: Funded 1 in 4 charter school startups in the U.S.
walton_family_foundation.total_grants_2023: Nearly $580 million in total grants dispensed
walton_family_foundation.colorado_example: Over $20 million to Colorado education advocacy groups (Colorado Succeeds, Colorado League of Charter Schools, Transform Education Now)
walton_family_foundation.source: Walton Family Foundation / Philanthropy Roundtable / Massachusetts Politics Professors

devos_family_education_politics.total_political_contributions: Betsy DeVos acknowledged under Senate questioning it was 'possible' the family gave $200 million to Republican candidates
devos_family_education_politics.documented_contributions: $82 million confirmed (Michigan state: $58M, federal: $17.7M, 12 other states: $6.4M)
devos_family_education_politics.annual_philanthropy: ~$90-94 million in total annual giving (2013-2014); nearly half to education groups
devos_family_education_politics.american_federation_for_children: Founded by DeVos; nation's largest school choice advocacy organization
devos_family_education_politics.source: NBC News / OpenSecrets / Michigan Campaign Finance Network / Snopes

democrats_for_education_reform.purpose: PAC encouraging Democratic Party support for charter schools and education reform
democrats_for_education_reform.walton_backing: Walton family has been major financial backer for over a decade
democrats_for_education_reform.2025_decline: Only Louisiana, New York, Texas, and D.C. chapters remain; staff reduced from 13 (January 2023) to 4 (February 2025)
democrats_for_education_reform.political_arm: Education Reform Now — received millions from Campaign for Great Public Schools (City Fund PAC)
democrats_for_education_reform.source: InfluenceWatch / Diane Ravitch / Bucks County Beacon

colorado_2024_example.pro_charter_lobbyists: Over 30 lobbyists hired to oppose charter accountability bill
colorado_2024_example.pro_charter_spending: $1.3 million to protect voucher schools from accountability

teachers_union_political_spending.overview: The NEA and AFT are among the most politically active organizations in the U.S., consistently ranking among top political donors. Their spending includes direct candidate contributions, independent expenditures, lobbying, and grants to advocacy organizations, with 97%+ going to Democrats.

advocacy_fund_pac_2024.top_recipients: Future Forward USA Action ($2.5M), House Majority PAC ($1.5M), Senate Majority PAC ($1.5M)

nea_national_education_association.source: OpenSecrets / The 74 / Americans for Fair Treatment

aft_american_federation_of_teachers.contributions_2024_cycle: Over $16 million to federal candidates (99.9% to Democrats)
aft_american_federation_of_teachers.political_activities_2024: $38 million+ on political activities and lobbying

combined_historical_spending.hard_money_1989_2009: $59.4 million — ranked #1 hard-money contributor to federal campaigns
combined_historical_spending.source: Defending Education / OpenSecrets / Capital Research Center

school_board_races.incumbent_win_rate_2024: 79-89% of incumbents seeking re-election won (2018-2024 range)
school_board_races.union_endorsement_impact: Union-endorsed candidates have historically won 60-71% of contested school board races
school_board_races.opposition_to_charter_voucher: Both NEA and AFT actively campaign against charter expansion and voucher programs at state and local levels

school_choice_voucher_movement.overview: The school choice movement experienced explosive growth from 2021-2025, with universal voucher/ESA programs going from zero to thirteen states. Backed by the Koch network, Heritage Foundation, and DeVos-founded AFC, the movement has reshaped education politics with tens of millions in coordinated spending.

american_federation_for_children.description: Nation's largest school choice advocacy organization
american_federation_for_children.laws_passed: Over 200 school choice laws across 31 states
american_federation_for_children.spending_2022: $9 million on state elections; won 277 of 368 races; defeated 40 incumbents
american_federation_for_children.texas_2024: Nearly $4 million spent; plans for $15 million nationally
american_federation_for_children.tennessee_2024: $1.125 million on lobbying (combined with AFP) for statewide voucher plan

koch_network.total_afp_funding: Nearly $80 million primarily from Koch Industries
koch_network.activities: Door-knocking, literature distribution, phone banking for pro-voucher candidates
koch_network.related_group: American Encore (Koch-sponsored) has spent millions on pro-voucher governors and ballot initiatives

heritage_foundation_project_2025.education_chapter_author: Lindsey Burke (17 years at Heritage; now DOE deputy chief of staff for policy and programs)
key_proposals: Phase out Title I funding for schools
Establish universal school choice / federal voucher program
Scale back federal involvement in education
heritage_foundation_project_2025.voucher_as_litmus_test: Heritage President Kevin Roberts identifies school vouchers as key litmus test for conservative presidential candidates
heritage_foundation_project_2025.source: Education Week / Heritage Foundation / Project 2025 Chapter 11

states_enacted_2025.texas: $10,000 per student ESA; $1 billion program for ~90,000 students; additional support for students with disabilities
states_enacted_2025.wyoming: Removed income restrictions; fully universal starting 2025-26
states_enacted_2025.idaho: New private school choice policy enacted

higher_education_lobbying.overview: Major research universities dramatically ramped up federal lobbying in 2025 in response to the Trump administration's attacks on higher education, including funding freezes, endowment tax hikes, DEI crackdowns, and threats to tax-exempt status. Total higher ed lobbying reached $37M+ in 2025.

harvard_lobbying_surge_2025.total_2025: Nearly $1 million — highest in over two decades
harvard_lobbying_surge_2025.q1_2025: $230,000 ($90,000 to Ballard Partners, firm with close Trump ties)
harvard_lobbying_surge_2025.trigger: Trump stripped Harvard of nearly $3 billion in federal funding; froze $2.2 billion within hours of Harvard defying demands

endowment_tax.original_rate: 1.4% excise tax on net investment income (Tax Cuts and Jobs Act 2017)
endowment_tax.new_rate_2025: Up to 8% for wealthiest private colleges (signed into law July 2025)
endowment_tax.harvard_impact: Could cost Harvard more than $200 million per year

trump_funding_threats_2025.trump_threat: Threatened to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status (April 2025)
trump_funding_threats_2025.universities_doubling_lobbying: Universities targeted by Trump more than doubled spending on lobbying

title_ix_regulation_battles.biden_2024_rule: Expanded sex-based harassment definition to include sexual orientation, gender identity, sex stereotypes
title_ix_regulation_battles.judicial_outcome: U.S. District Court (Eastern District of Kentucky) vacated Biden's 2024 Title IX rule — found it violated First Amendment and Spending Clause

dei_affirmative_action_litigation.sffa_v_harvard_2023: Supreme Court struck down race-conscious admissions (June 2023)
dei_affirmative_action_litigation.executive_orders_jan_2025: EO 14151 and EO 14173 dismantled DEI programs across federal agencies and contractors
dei_affirmative_action_litigation.state_anti_dei_laws: Alabama law prohibiting public college DEI offices took effect October 2024; multiple states enacted similar legislation
dei_affirmative_action_litigation.litigation_groups: America First Legal filed suits against universities (e.g., NYU October 2023) alleging Title VI/IX violations in DEI-influenced selection processes
dei_affirmative_action_litigation.funding_threats: Institutions with $1B+ endowments face investigations under executive order

textbook_edtech_industry.overview: The textbook and education technology industry is dominated by a few major players — Pearson, McGraw-Hill, and the College Board — that wield significant market power. The College Board operates as a de facto monopoly in standardized testing while maintaining nonprofit status and generating over $1 billion in annual revenue.

college_board.monopoly_concerns: Only organization distributing PSAT, SAT, and AP exams; deployed lobbyists to block competitor entrance into college entrance exam market
college_board.antitrust_argument: Columbia Undergraduate Law Review published case for Sherman Act Section 2 enforcement
college_board.source: Washington Examiner / College Board tax filings / Total Registration / CULR

pearson.state_lobbying: Deployed 6 lobbyists at Texas Capitol alone
pearson.market_position: World's largest education company; dominates textbook and assessment markets
pearson.mcgraw_hill_partnership: Announced new partnership (July 2025) bringing Pearson assessment into McGraw-Hill K-12 curriculum
pearson.source: OpenSecrets / Texas Observer / EdTech Innovation Hub

edtech_venture_capital.political_influence: VC-backed EdTech investors conduct studies, issue reports, organize networking, work with policymakers as economic and political actors
edtech_venture_capital.source: EdWeek Market Brief / Higher Ed Dive / HolonIQ

department_of_education.overview: The Trump administration has pursued the most aggressive effort in history to dismantle the Department of Education, cutting nearly half its workforce, transferring programs to other agencies, and signing legislation aligned with Project 2025's education chapter authored by a now-DOE official.

elimination_efforts_2025.executive_order: Signed March 2025 to begin dismantling the department
elimination_efforts_2025.workforce_reduction: ~1,700 employees cut — nearly 50% of workforce relative to Inauguration Day
elimination_efforts_2025.ocr_impact: At least 240 Office for Civil Rights employees laid off, most of them attorneys investigating complaints
elimination_efforts_2025.ies_cuts: DOGE cut dozens of IES research contracts worth roughly $900 million
elimination_efforts_2025.program_transfers: Six agreements to move programs (including Title I) to other agencies, with Department of Labor taking over some largest K-12 funding streams
elimination_efforts_2025.source: NPR / Bloomberg / CNN / Center for American Progress

budget_proposal_fy2026.essa_consolidation: 18 programs consolidated into one with $4.5 billion fewer dollars (-70%)
budget_proposal_fy2026.idea_consolidation: Individuals with Disabilities Education funding consolidated into one program
budget_proposal_fy2026.eliminated: All federal funding for Language Instruction for English Learners

lindsey_burke.from: Heritage Foundation (17 years; Director of Center for Education Policy)
lindsey_burke.to: DOE Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy and Programs
lindsey_burke.note: Co-authored Project 2025 education chapter proposing the department's dismantlement, then hired to implement it

revolving_door.devos_era_pattern: Education Secretary Betsy DeVos staffed DOE with former for-profit college executives who then rolled back regulations on that industry
revolving_door.lobbying_the_doe_2024: 403 clients lobbied the Department of Education in 2024

education_secretary_linda_mcmahon.strategy: Working within legal constraints to dismantle DOE from inside — following Project 2025 blueprint
education_secretary_linda_mcmahon.approach: Eliminate programs deemed ineffective/duplicative, shift functions to other agencies, transfer funding control to states

early_childhood_education.overview: Early childhood education faces a critical funding moment. The expiration of $52.5 billion in pandemic-era child care funding created a 'cliff' threatening millions of children's care, while Head Start has faced elimination threats under Project 2025. Advocacy groups mounted massive grassroots campaigns to preserve funding.

advocacy_response.activities: Rallies nationwide, direct visits to Republican members, White House outreach

head_start.outcome_2025: Head Start received reprieve from Trump budget cuts, but fight ongoing

pandemic_funding_expired.total: $52.5 billion in pandemic-era child care funding expired

sector_impact_2024.causes: Staffing shortages (89%), low pay (77%), lack of affordability for families (66%)

child_care_funding_cliff.source: Century Foundation / NAEYC / Pew Charitable Trusts

state_pre_k_spending.total_2023_2024: Over $13.6 billion (including $257 million in federal COVID relief)
state_pre_k_spending.source: National Institute for Early Education Research (NIEER)

universal_pre_k_federal_efforts.biden_push: Urged states to use existing funds to expand preschool (February 2024) without new federal money
universal_pre_k_federal_efforts.murray_bill: Sen. Patty Murray introduced bill to extend child care funding; delivered $1 billion increase for FY2024

key_patterns_and_connections.education_as_ideological_battleground: Education policy has become one of the most politically polarized spending areas, with conservative billionaires (Koch, DeVos, Walton, Yass) funding voucher expansion while teachers unions spend hundreds of millions defending public education
key_patterns_and_connections.revolving_door_cycle: Heritage Foundation authors write Project 2025 education chapter, then get hired into DOE to implement it; for-profit college executives join DOE under DeVos then deregulate their former industry
key_patterns_and_connections.asymmetric_spending: In state-level charter/voucher battles, pro-privatization forces routinely outspend opponents by 100:1 or more (e.g., Colorado's 158:1 ratio in 2024)
key_patterns_and_connections.student_debt_as_political_weapon: The $1.7T+ student debt crisis drives lobbying from servicers protecting their revenue, legal challenges from states blocking forgiveness, and competing political narratives from both parties
key_patterns_and_connections.consolidation_of_testing_market: College Board maintains effective monopoly on college admissions testing with $1B+ revenue while actively lobbying to block competitors, all under nonprofit tax-exempt status
key_patterns_and_connections.universal_voucher_acceleration: From zero universal voucher states in 2021 to thirteen by 2025, representing the fastest policy diffusion in modern education history, driven by coordinated spending from AFC, Koch network, and Heritage Foundation
